perturbation.original_text: WHEREAS, for the purpose stated in Section 2 below, Company and Amphenol (collectively referred to as the “Parties” and individually referred to as a “Party”) have determined to establish terms governing the use and protection of Confidential Information (as defined in Section 1 below) that one Party (“Owner”) may disclose to the other Party (“the Recipient”).
perturbation.changed_text: WHEREAS, for the purpose stated in Section 2 below, Company and Amphenol (collectively referred to as the “Parties” and individually referred to as a “Party”) have determined to establish terms governing the use and protection of Confidential Information (as defined in Section 1 below) that one Party (“Disclosing Party”) may disclose to the other Party (“the Receiver”).
perturbation.explanation: The terms 'Owner' and 'Recipient' were previously defined as the party disclosing and receiving information respectively. Changing these terms to 'Disclosing Party' and 'Receiver' without updating the rest of the contract introduces ambiguity. This is contradicting the original agreed upon definitions, this creates confusion, because it is not clear that 'Owner' is the same as 'Disclosing Party' and 'Recipient' is the same as 'Receiver'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Recipient shall protect such Confidential Information from disclosure to others, using the same degree of care used to protect its own confidential or proprietary information of like importance, but in any case using no less than a reasonable degree of care. The Recipient may disclose Confidential Information received hereunder to (i) its Affiliates, to its employees and agents, and its Affiliates’ employees and agents, who have a need to know the Confidential Information, and who agree to protect the Confidential Information from unauthorized use and disclosure. Confidential Information shall be used solely for the Purpose and shall not otherwise be disclosed to any third party without the prior written consent of the Owner.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Owner' with 'Disclosing Party' and 'Recipient' with 'Receiver' without updating the contradicted text. The contradicted text explicitly uses the term 'Recipient' and 'Owner', which are no longer defined after the change. This creates a strong in-text contradiction because the original definitions are altered, making the contradicted section unclear and potentially unenforceable. The 'Misaligned Terminology' leads to an in-text contradiction as the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations. Therefore the change breaks compatibility with the contradicted section.

perturbation.original_text: Confidential Information disclosed hereunder is intended to be used for the purpose of discussing the following general business opportunities: (the “Purpose”).
perturbation.changed_text: Confidential Information disclosed hereunder is intended to be used for the following general business opportunities: (the “Objective”).
perturbation.explanation: Changing the term 'Purpose' to 'Objective' without clarifying that they are the same thing creates confusion and contradicts the intended use of Confidential Information. The term Objective is used throughout the document, and the document does not clarify whether they are the same. This contradicts section 9.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party agrees to protect Confidential Information received from the other by exercising, at a minimum, the same level of care it uses to protect its own confidential information and agrees that it will (a) use such Confidential Information only for the purposes of this Agreement; (b) only make such Confidential Information available to its employees having a “need to know”; (c) not disclose the Confidential Information to any third person, except that Confidential Information may be disclosed to the demand of the Government (d) not copy or reproduce Confidential Information in whole or in part, except as necessary to carry out the purposes of this Agreement, pr ovided that, if they do copy or reproduce Confidential Information in whole or in part, the reproduction shall carry a proprietary notice or legend similar to that which appears on the original; (e) not manufacture, sell, use, or have made devices embodying or utilizing Confidential In formation, except as otherwise agreed to by the Parties in writing.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Purpose' with 'Objective' in defining the use of Confidential Information. The contradicted text explicitly states that Confidential Information should be used 'only for the purposes of this Agreement'. By changing 'Purpose' to 'Objective' without clarifying they are the same, it creates ambiguity about whether 'Objective' aligns with 'purposes of this Agreement'. This misalignment introduces a contradiction as it is unclear if using Confidential Information for the 'Objective' fulfills the obligation to use it solely for the 'purposes of this Agreement'. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' because the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: The Recipient shall protect such Confidential Information from disclosure to others, using the same degree of care used to protect its own confidential or proprietary information of like importance, but in any case using no less than a reasonable degree of care.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiver shall protect such Confidential Information from disclosure to others, using a commercially reasonable degree of care.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the standard of care from 'the same degree of care' (but no less than reasonable) to a 'commercially reasonable degree of care' alters the obligation and introduces a conflicting standard. The original reasonable degree of care is now being replaced by a standard the company agrees upon, where it is not clear if it will be less or more than the original, contradicting section 9, about the reasonable duty of care of keeping confidentiality
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party agrees to protect Confidential Information received from the other by exercising, at a minimum, the same level of care it uses to protect its own confidential information and agrees that it will (a) use such Confidential Information only for the purposes of this Agreement; (b) only make such Confidential Information available to its employees having a “need to know”; (c) not disclose the Confidential Information to any third person, except that Confidential Information may be disclosed to the demand of the Government (d) not copy or reproduce Confidential Information in whole or in part, except as necessary to carry out the purposes of this Agreement, pr ovided that, if they do copy or reproduce Confidential Information in whole or in part, the reproduction shall carry a proprietary notice or legend similar to that which appears on the original; (e) not manufacture, sell, use, or have made devices embodying or utilizing Confidential In formation, except as otherwise agreed to by the Parties in writing.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified a degree of care for Confidential Information, ensuring at least 'a reasonable degree of care'. The changed text replaces this with 'a commercially reasonable degree of care'. This contradicts the contradicted text because it alters the explicitly defined standard of care. The contradicted text specifically states that each party must protect confidential information by exercising at minimum, the same level of care it uses to protect its own. Changing to 'commercially reasonable' introduces potential ambiguity and a potentially lower standard of care, directly conflicting with the original requirement to maintain 'at a minimum, the same level of care' the company uses to protect its own confidential information. This makes the obligations unclear, which fits the definition of Misaligned Terminology.

perturbation.original_text: All Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement (including without limitation, information in computer software or held in electronic storage media) shall be and remain the property of the Owner.
perturbation.changed_text: All Confidential Data disclosed under this Agreement (including without limitation, information in computer software or held in electronic storage media) shall be and remain the property of the Holder.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Confidential Data' and 'Owner' to 'Holder' introduces uncertainty and contradicts previous uses of the original terms. This contradicts section 8, of the obligations of the owner regarding the Confidential Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Owner represents that it has the authority to disclose to the Recipient any Confidential Information, which it elects to disclose hereunder. The Owner shall not, however, have any liability or responsibility for errors or omissions in, or any decisions made by the Recipient in reliance on, any Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies 'Confidential Information' to 'Confidential Data' and 'Owner' to 'Holder.' The contradicted text refers specifically to the obligations and representations of the 'Owner' regarding 'Confidential Information.' By changing these terms, the changed text introduces ambiguity and creates a direct conflict, because the obligations of the 'Owner' with respect to 'Confidential Information' are no longer clearly defined or applicable. This aligns with the definition of Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction, where the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations. Therefore, the change creates a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: No licenses or rights under any patent, copyright, trademark, or trade secret are granted or are to be implied by this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: No licenses or rights under any patent, copyright, intellectual property, or trade secret are granted or are to be implied by this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'trademark' with 'intellectual property' broadens the scope and creates ambiguity as to whether specific trademark rights are still excluded, leading to a contradiction in the implied rights. By replacing trademark rights with the broader intellectual property, it is not clear what rights remain protected, contradicting the original protections set in place in Section 9.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party agrees to protect Confidential Information received from the other by exercising, at a minimum, the same level of care it uses to protect its own confidential information and agrees that it will (a) use such Confidential Information only for the purposes of this Agreement; (b) only make such Confidential Information available to its employees having a “need to know”; (c) not disclose the Confidential Information to any third person, except that Confidential Information may be disclosed to the demand of the Government (d) not copy or reproduce Confidential Information in whole or in part, except as necessary to carry out the purposes of this Agreement, pr ovided that, if they do copy or reproduce Confidential Information in whole or in part, the reproduction shall carry a proprietary notice or legend similar to that which appears on the original; (e) not manufacture, sell, use, or have made devices embodying or utilizing Confidential In formation, except as otherwise agreed to by the Parties in writing.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifically excluded rights under 'trademark'. The changed text broadens this exclusion to 'intellectual property', which encompasses trademarks. The contradicted text requires protection of confidential information, which could include trademarks. By removing 'trademark' specifically and replacing it with the broad 'intellectual property', it creates a contradiction about whether specific trademark rights are still being protected, conflicting with the clause that requires all confidential information be protected.

perturbation.original_text: Each party agrees to protect Confidential Information received from the other by exercising, at a minimum, the same level of care it uses to protect its own confidential information and agrees that it will (a) use such Confidential Information only for the purposes of this Agreement; (b) only make such Confidential Information available to its employees having a “need to know”; (c) not disclose the Confidential Information to any third person, except that Confidential Information may be disclosed to the demand of the Government (d) not copy or reproduce Confidential Information in whole or in part, except as necessary to carry out the purposes of this Agreement, pr ovided that, if they do copy or reproduce Confidential Information in whole or in part, the reproduction shall carry a proprietary notice or legend similar to that which appears on the original; (e) not manufacture, sell, use, or have made devices embodying or utilizing Confidential In formation, except as otherwise agreed to by the Parties in writing.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party agrees to protect Sensitive Information received from the other by exercising, at a minimum, the same level of care it uses to protect its own sensitive information and agrees that it will (a) use such Sensitive Information only for the Objectives of this Agreement; (b) only make such Sensitive Information available to its personnel having a “need to know”; (c) not disclose the Sensitive Information to any outside party, except that Sensitive Information may be disclosed to the demand of the Government (d) not copy or reproduce Sensitive Information in whole or in part, except as necessary to carry out the Objectives of this Agreement, pr ovided that, if they do copy or reproduce Sensitive Information in whole or in part, the reproduction shall carry a proprietary notice or legend similar to that which appears on the original; (e) not manufacture, sell, use, or have made devices embodying or utilizing Sensitive Information, except as otherwise agreed to by the Parties in writing.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Sensitive Information', 'employees' to 'personnel', 'third person' to 'outside party', and 'purposes' to 'Objectives' introduces multiple inconsistencies. There is no definition of outside party to define if it is the same as third person. Also by saying Sensitive Information and Objective create contradictoins with the term, 'Confidential Information' and 'Purpose' since there were the original defined ones. This contradicts the original defined terms from the document, since now the reader doesn't know what to assume, it could be the original terms, or these new terms in this section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: “Confidential Information” means information that relates to the purpose stated in Section 2 below that has been designated as proprietary by the disclosing party with an appropriate conspicuous stamp or legend on the document exchanged or, although not related to such purpose, is nevertheless disclosed, and that should reasonably have been understood by the Recipient, because of appropriate and conspicuous legends or other markings, to be proprietary and confidential to the Owner, an Affiliate of the Owner or to a third party. The term “Affiliate” means any person or entity directly or indirectly controlling controlled by, or under common control with a Party. Confidential Information may be disclosed in written or other tangible form (including magnetic media) or by oral, visual or other means. In addition, information and data disclosed orally shall be subject to the provisions of this Agreement provided; (a) the disclosing party clearly identifies the orally disclosed data as proprietary at the time of disclosure, and (b) such orally disclosed data is subsequently reduced to writing, marked with an appropriate stamp or legend and provided in writing as Confidential Information pursuant to Article 2 within ten (10) days of such oral disclosures.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Confidential Information' to 'Sensitive Information', 'employees' to 'personnel', 'third person' to 'outside party', and 'purposes' to 'Objectives', creates a strong in-text contradiction with the contradicted text's definition of 'Confidential Information' and 'purpose'. The contradicted text defines 'Confidential Information' in relation to the 'purpose stated in Section 2'. By changing these terms in the changed text, the document now contains conflicting terms that refer to the same concept but are defined differently or not defined at all, creating ambiguity and inconsistency, according to the Misaligned Terminology definition. The definition of outside party is never introduced.
